seo.title: Building Workflow Automation with LlamaIndex + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build workflow automation with LlamaIndex + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Workflow Automation with LlamaIndex enables multi-step agents that retrieve indexed data, make API calls, and handle conditional logic for complex tasks. Guardrails are essential to prevent irrelevant retrievals, hallucinations, outdated data, and chunking errors that can derail workflows. Vex provides runtime verification to ensure action correctness, permissions, and rollback safety in these high-stakes automations.

architectureOverview.heading: Workflow Automation Architecture with LlamaIndex
architectureOverview.description:
The architecture starts with LlamaIndex indexing enterprise documents and APIs into vector stores for RAG retrieval. A central workflow orchestrator uses query engines to fetch relevant context, then feeds it to an LLM agent that executes multi-step tasks: API integrations (CRM, payments), conditional branching based on retrieved data, and human-in-the-loop approvals via webhooks.

The agent maintains state across steps, with tools for database queries, external API calls, and document synthesis. LlamaIndex's query engines handle retrieval while ReAct-style agents manage tool execution. Vex wraps critical decision points to verify outputs before execution.

Risks emerge when retrieval fails to provide query-relevant context, causing the synthesizer to hallucinate actions, or when index staleness leads to outdated API decisions.

risks.name: Retrieved context irrelevant to query
risks.description: LlamaIndex retrieves semantically similar but topically irrelevant chunks, causing agent to execute wrong workflow branch
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies retrieved context semantic similarity threshold before agent planning

risks.name: Synthesizer hallucinating beyond source documents
risks.description: LLM fabricates API endpoints or parameters not present in retrieved context
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation ensures generated actions match predefined tool schemas

risks.name: Index staleness causing outdated responses
risks.description: Workflow executes against expired pricing/policy data from stale index
risks.mitigation: Vex temporal validation checks document freshness before critical actions

risks.name: Chunk boundary errors losing critical context
risks.description: Important workflow instructions split across chunk boundaries, causing incomplete agent reasoning
risks.mitigation: Vex context completeness scoring ensures all referenced entities appear in retrieved chunks

implementation.title: Basic LlamaIndex Query Engine Setup
implementation.description: Initialize vector index and query engine for workflow context retrieval
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core import VectorStoreIndex, SimpleDirectoryReader, Settings
from llama_index.llms.openai import OpenAI

# Configure LLM and embeddings
Settings.llm = OpenAI(model="gpt-4")
Settings.embed_model = "text-embedding-3-small"

documents = SimpleDirectoryReader("workflow_docs").load_data()
index = VectorStoreIndex.from_documents(documents)
query_engine = index.as_query_engine(
    similarity_top_k=5,
    node_postprocessors=[
        SimilarityPostprocessor(similarity_cutoff=0.8)
    ]
)

implementation.title: Workflow Agent with Tool Integration
implementation.description: Create ReAct agent with API tools for multi-step execution
implementation.code:
from llama_index.core.agent import ReActAgent
from llama_index.core.tools import FunctionTool

async def approve_request(amount: float, user_id: str) -> str:
    return f"Approved ${amount} for user {user_id}"

approval_tool = FunctionTool.from_defaults(fn=approve_request)
agent = ReActAgent.from_tools([approval_tool], llm=Settings.llm, verbose=True)
response = await agent.chat("Process $500 payment for user-123 with approval")
